title: brachiaria decumbens var. ruziziensis
intro: Brachiaria decumbens var. ruziziensis, also known as Ruzi grass or Elephant grass, is a tropical grass species native to Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ruzi grass include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions, making it a valuable resource in traditional African medicine. Herbal preparations of Ruzi grass can be made by infusing its leaves in water or boiling them to create a tea, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
